I'm now contemplating the behavior of the "Select All" function within `SalesManager.tsx`. Currently, it selects all filtered orders, which spans across all pages, as indicated in the implementation. I'm focusing on ensuring this aligns with expected user behavior in admin dashboards, considering that users often expect "Select All" to apply to the visible items on the current page. I'll need to consider this in light of the new pagination.


**Evaluating Select All Behavior**

I'm now determining the most appropriate "Select All" behavior within the paginated context of `SalesManager.tsx`. My instinct is to stick with selecting all filtered orders, given this functionality is more powerful, but it may lead to UX concerns. I've re-evaluated the checkbox state, and it will be checked only when all filtered orders across all pages are selected. To avoid confusion, I'm considering adding a note to explain this behavior. I'm also considering changing the logic to select only the current page, which is more typical in paginated interfaces. I need to weigh the benefits of simplicity and convention against the existing bulk selection use case.


**Confirming Lucide Imports**

I've decided to maintain the `toggleSelectAll` functionality to operate across all filtered orders. The `handleBulkCopy` function relies on this behavior. **Refactoring Pagination Calculations**

I'm now refactoring pagination logic in `SalesManager.tsx`. I've realized `filteredOrders` is derived within the render function. I'm adjusting the calculation of `totalPages` and slicing `filteredOrders` to derive `paginatedOrders` within the render to accommodate this. I'll maintain the `itemsPerPage` constant for readability.


**Implementing Page Navigation**

I'm now making final adjustments to the page navigation within `SalesManager.tsx`. I've implemented the `goToPage` handler, ensuring it correctly updates the `currentPage` state. I'm also now linking the `paginatedOrders.map` to display the right orders in the UI. I have to ensure pagination controls work correctly.


**Finalizing Pagination UI**

I've just added the display showing the order range and page numbers, as well as the left and right chevron buttons. The button's `disabled` state works as expected, and the UI elements are using the desired styling. Everything's working as intended, and I'm ready to move on.

I'm removing the stock check in `firebase.ts`' `createOrderInDb` and allowing stock to go negative, as intended. I'm updating `types.ts` to add `isPreOrder` to `CartItem`. I'll also modify `App.tsx` and the other components as described.


**Modifying UI Elements**

I'm now focusing on implementing the visual changes in the UI components, specifically `ProductCard.tsx`, `ProductDetails.tsx`, `CartView.tsx`, and `CheckoutView.tsx`. I am focusing on the product card badge and button in `ProductCard.tsx`, updating the text, badge, and descriptive text. I am also updating the button text, adding a pre-order indicator, and descriptive text in `ProductDetails.tsx`, `CartView.tsx`, and `CheckoutView.tsx`. Finally, I will integrate the `isPreOrder` flag in the shopping cart and checkout views.


**Adjusting Quantity Logic**

I'm now refining how the quantity is handled with the new pre-order functionality. My thought is, if the stock is greater than zero, I will continue to limit quantity to the available stock. But, if the stock is zero or less, I'll allow unlimited quantity (or a reasonable maximum). This aligns with the "pre-order" concept; I am now revising `ProductDetails.tsx` to handle this logic.

I will update the application to enable pre-orders when a product is out of stock.

Here are the changes:
1.  **`types.ts`**: Add an `isPreOrder` flag to `CartItem`.
2.  **`firebase.ts`**: Modify the `createOrderInDb` transaction to allow stock to go negative (representing pre-orders) instead of throwing an error when stock is insufficient.
3.  **`App.tsx`**: Update the `handleAddToCart` logic to detect if an item is being added while out of stock and flag it as a pre-order.
4.  **`components/ProductCard.tsx`**: Replace the "Sold Out" state with a "Pre-order" state, changing the badge and enabling the button.
5.  **`components/ProductDetails.tsx`**: Enable the "Add to Bag" button even when stock is zero, change the text to "Pre-order", and add a notification about the 2-week shipping time.
6.  **`components/CartView.tsx` & `components/CheckoutView.tsx`**: Display a visual indicator for pre-order items so the customer is aware of the shipping delay.

entries.description: Added isPreOrder field to CartItem interface.
Allowed stock to go negative in createOrderInDb to support pre-orders.
Flag cart items as pre-orders if stock is less than or equal to zero during addition.
Replaced 'Sold Out' logic with 'Pre-order' logic, including badge updates and enabling the add button.
Updated product details to allow adding pre-orders with a specific notification message.
Added visual indicator for pre-order items in the cart.

import React from 'react'; import { CartItem } from '../types'; import { Minus, Plus, Trash2, ArrowRight, ArrowLeft, Gift, PenTool, Clock } from 'lucide-react'; import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; interface CartViewProps { cart: CartItem[]; onUpdateQuantity: (id: string, delta: number) => void; onRemoveItem: (id: string) => void; } export const CartView: React.FC = ({ cart, onUpdateQuantity, onRemoveItem }) => { const navigate = useNavigate(); const subtotal = cart.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.price * item.quantity), 0); const totalItems = cart.reduce((sum, item) => sum + item.quantity, 0); // Packaging Logic: 1 set for every 1 item const packagingCount = totalItems; if (cart.length === 0) { return (

Your bag is empty

Discover our collection of signature pieces.

); } return (

Shopping Bag

{/* Cart Items List */}
{cart.map((item) => (
{/* Image */}
{item.isPreOrder && (
Pre-order
)}
{/* Details */}

{item.name}

{item.collection || 'Blankets'}

{item.isPreOrder && (

Ships in 2 Weeks

)}

RM {item.price}

{/* Quantity Control */}
{item.quantity}
{/* Remove */}
))}
{/* Order Summary */}

Order Summary

{/* Complimentary Touches Section */}

Complimentary Touches

{/* Item 1: Box */}

Signature Keepsake Box

x {packagingCount}
{/* Item 2: Card */}

Gift Card

x {packagingCount}

*One set included for every item.

Subtotal RM {subtotal}
Shipping Calculated at checkout
Total
RM {subtotal} + Shipping

Including taxes & duties

Secure checkout powered by CHIP.

); };
